notes: Running the game fails on the 1st try. You then need to change the Windows version on the Wine prefix to WinXP. On the 2nd run it should install dotnet 3.5 just fine and eventually run. You might get some stuttering from shader cashing for a while. Just keep playing.
title: Batman: Arkham City - Game of the Year Edition

notes: DX9 everything runs pretty normal without fuss, sometimes it runs minimized so a click on the taskbar and I'm in the game. DX11 I sometimes have to run the game twice to get to see the picture. first run I can hear it in the background but for whatever reason it takes two launches. anyways, like many others said you need to run winecfg and set it to winxp. this is just to get .net to install properly (took a long time, like 5+ minutes). The game actually launches fine without this but it's annoying seeing the "first time setup" thing each run. after .net is installed you can set it back to win7 or I have it set to 10. it probably doesn't make a difference but I can't imagine XP running this game better than 7 or 10 so YMMV.
title: Batman: Arkham City - Game of the Year Edition

notes: Here is what I did to make it runs: 1)Install "protontricks". 2)Run this command "protontricks 200260 winxp". 3)Run this command "protontricks 200260 dotnet20 vcrun2005 dsound dmusic mdx d3dx9 d3dcompiler_43 win10". 4)Use this launch option to run the game "PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%". 5)Try to run the game, just click OK when an error message appear. 6)It takes a while before the game menu appear, just be patient. Physx performance is very bad, better left it off. That is why I didn't install physx. Original solution comes from here : https://lutris.net/games/install/1866/view
title: Batman: Arkham City - Game of the Year Edition

notes: First, my driver is NOT what proton reports, it is Nvidia 415.27. Been having issues with protontricks so I used an explict prefix for the steamlibrary where the game is and ran "WINEPREFIX=/pub/SteamLibrary/SteamApps/compatdata/200260/pfx WINEARCH=win64 wine winecfg" to reset the wine version to XP and then reran the game. It should succesfully install .NET after that and on my system so far, its not encountering any major issues. Gameplay is smooth and there's no video artifacts.
title: Batman: Arkham City - Game of the Year Edition

notes: (Please disregard previous report, there was outdated system information) I used the fix to set the cfg to winxp and after that the game installed .NET successfully and runs fine so far.
title: Batman: Arkham City - Game of the Year Edition

gpu: NVIDIA Quadro P2000 with Max-Q Design
notes:
1. Do a first run that fails (this intitializes the Proton prefix)
2. Run `protontricks 200260 winxp`
3. Run `protontricks 200260 dotnet20 vcrun2005 dsound dmusic mdx d3dx9 d3dcompiler_43 win10`
4. Profit

NOTES:
The "Configure PC Options" screen only runs with without DXVK (PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1). The game itself runs well with DXVK, as long as no DX11 features are enabled in the graphics options. DX11 features do work with PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1. Test yourself to see if you want the DXVK speedup or the DX11 graphics features (MVSS, HBAO, and tessellation).

title: Batman: Arkham City - Game of the Year Edition

notes: Works nicely with good FPS (in DX9 mode) on Proton 4.11, 175 FPS as average, use "PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 PROTON_NO_D3D10=1 %command%". The automated benchmark stutters heavily with lowest FPS near zero when using OpenGL ( PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 ). I successfully ran it in DX10/DX11 mode without stutter and around 50 FPS less as average.
title: Batman: Arkham City - Game of the Year Edition

notes: Like Batman Arkham Asylum, here we need to use protontricks again, only now in this game (protontricks 200260 mdx d3dx9 d3dcompiler_43 win10). This done, just "force" the use of Proton 4.11 and the commands at game startup (PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 PROTON_NO_D3D10=1% command%). That done just enjoy the game. Without d9vk, the game in the HIGH setting will average 42 FPS. Already with d9vk, the average was at the subscript 70fps, reaching 170 at some points. Improvement with the d9vk is undeniable.
title: Batman: Arkham City - Game of the Year Edition

notes: Game is playable on full-hd, DX11, no tessellation, max settings, but stutters a little bit at the beginning of new areas/scenes (with tessellation, the stuttering is severe).

title: Batman: Arkham City - Game of the Year Edition

notes:
This may seem strange, but it works for me. I click on the "Play" button in Steam and the preparatory window opens and then I can hear the main menu music but nothing else appears i.e. the main menu doesn't show up and I'm still looking at Steam. Then clicking the "Play" button a second time, while the main menu music is still playing in the background the prep window appears again then the main game window shows up with the main menu ready to go. Clicking on the "Click Here to Start" button in the game takes me to the main layout to select the saved game I want to play then I can continue in the game as normal. 

I'm using the latest version of Proton, which is 4.11-3 and is not shown in here which is why I have just selected the default version. There is a bit of jerkiness during the combat scenes but this is minimal and nothing that I worry about. It doesn't alter the game play in anyway.

title: Batman: Arkham City - Game of the Year Edition

notes:
The game launched perfect out of the box with all graphic settings default no NVIDIA PhysX. Running around the city, my frames was stuck around 50 and frequent stutter FPS drops. After reading a report, I decided to put these commands in launch options:

PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 PROTON_NO_D3D10=1 %command%

I don't know if that fixed my frame rate issue, or I just needed a game restart. Either way, the game should launch and run fine with or without these launch commands.
title: Batman: Arkham City - Game of the Year Edition
